text:
Deep learning (DL) has become a dominant approach in many areas of decision making at all levels based on evidence from the data. It is a subset of machine learning that has demonstrated several advantages such as ability to learn features at different abstraction levels from the data using a hierarchical non-linear mapping. It has been widely used in many fields such as healthcare, security and finance among others. In spite of its numerous applications, the field is still growing and therefore, reviews of the various deep learning architectures have not been exhaustively carried out. This work is a comprehensive review of the recent developments of the models, their evolutions, applications and their strengths and weaknesses.

Abstract A broad spectrum of deep learning architectures is identified, classified and examined within this paper. Well known structures such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) and Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN), as well as lesser known networks such as Deep Belief Networks (DBN), Autoencoders, and Capsule Networks are elaborated. The use of hybrid models in order to mitigate some drawbacks of the basic models, such as improving the robustness of models, as well as handling different domains and tasks, is discussed. Finally, the problems that need to be solved, such as long training times, large amounts of training data, poor classification accuracy and getting deep neural networks to converge to the optimum solution are elaborated. A review of the current research, as well as the future works and a focus on capsule networks is presented in this survey, which aims at assisting researchers, practitioners and organizations about the deep learning architectures that could be used for the development and the implementation of new systems and applications using deep learning.
